steps: An object launched upward follows a parabolic path: height increases until the peak (vertex), then decreases. The graph shows the vertex at \(x=2\).
For a downward-opening parabola, height is increasing for \(x < 2\) and decreasing for \(x > 2\).
An interval where height is increasing for the ENTIRE interval must lie entirely to the left of the vertex (\(x=2\)).
explanation:
• Choice A — 'From \(x=0\) to \(x=2\)': The entire interval is at or before the vertex. The object is rising throughout \([0,2]\). ✓
  • Choice B — 'From \(x=0\) to \(x=4\)': The object peaks at \(x=2\) and is falling from \(x=2\) to \(x=4\), so height is NOT increasing for the entire interval. ✗
  • Choice C — 'From \(x=2\) to \(x=3\)': \(x=2\) is the vertex (maximum). From that point onward the object is falling, so height is not increasing on this interval. ✗
  • Choice D — 'From \(x=3\) to \(x=4\)': This is after the peak — the object is falling the entire time. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
